name: Southwest® Rapid Rewards® Premier Credit Card
effective_cost: Medium — the balanced pick if you want A-List progress without Priority's fee

verdict.text: Best-balanced tier — Preferred seating, real A-List progress

earn_note: Same 1× base as Plus and Priority, but the bonus category shifts to groceries + restaurants (from Plus's gas + groceries) and the Southwest multiplier climbs to 3×. Still nothing for Chase Travel or non-Southwest travel spend.

points.note: Same closed-loop Rapid Rewards currency and dynamic pricing (since March 2025) as the rest of the family — this tier only changes how fast you earn it, not what it's worth.

transfer_partners.highlight: Rapid Rewards points only redeem for Southwest travel (or a short list of gift-card/merchandise partners at poor value) — there's no way to move them to another airline or hotel program.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: Closed-loop currency, unchanged in structure across all three Southwest personal cards. Chase Ultimate Rewards can convert into this account, but nothing moves out of it.

credits.name: Free Checked Bag
credits.subtitle: 1st bag · you + up to 8 on reservation
credits.description: Southwest ended free checked bags for everyone on May 28, 2025 ($35 first bag / $45 second bag on most fares). Rapid Rewards cardmembers keep their first checked bag free — for the cardholder and up to 8 companions on the same reservation.
tips: Only the first bag is free on this card — Southwest's own A-List Preferred members and Choice Extra fares get a free second bag too; this tier doesn't.
It covers everyone on the reservation, not just the cardholder — a family checking one bag each on a round trip can clear a big chunk of the $149 fee in a single trip.

credits.name: Instacart+ Credit
credits.subtitle: $10/mo · through 2027 promo
credits.description: Up to $10/month in Instacart credit on one qualifying $10+ order, available with an active Instacart+ membership (3 months complimentary) — added in the July 2025 refresh, running through the Dec 31, 2027 promo window.
tips: Requires enrolling in Instacart+ (free for the first 3 months, a paid membership after) — only worth carrying if you'd keep the subscription anyway.
The monthly credit doesn't roll over — a month skipped is a month lost.

insurance.detail: Secondary (domestic) / primary outside the US · $60k · 31 days
Not offered on any Southwest personal card

protection_note: Identical protections to Plus and Priority — Chase doesn't scale insurance with the fee on this family. No trip cancellation/interruption coverage at any tier; the $500,000 travel accident policy and baggage delay/loss coverage are what you get regardless of which of the three you carry.
rental_note: Secondary for domestic rentals (pays after your own auto policy), primary only outside the US, capped at $60,000 and 31 consecutive days. Decline the counter's CDW and pay with the card, but don't rely on it as your only coverage for a US rental.

status_perks.name: Companion Pass progress
status_perks.note: A 10,000-point boost toward the 135,000 Companion Pass qualifying points needed each year — identical across all three Southwest cards, so it's not a reason to pick this tier over Plus or Priority.

status_perks.name: A-List tier points
status_perks.note: 1,500 Tier Qualifying Points per $5,000 spent — a real assist toward the 35,000-TQP A-List bar if you already run a lot of spend through this card, though Priority earns them faster (2,500 per $5,000).

status_perks.name: Boarding position
status_perks.note: Group 5 boarding for the cardholder + up to 8 companions on the reservation — same slot as Plus and Priority. Real A-List members still board ahead of all three in the A1–A15 range.

services.name: Seat selection
services.detail: Complimentary Preferred seat selection within 48 hours of departure, when available, for you + up to 8 companions — a step up from Plus's Standard-only seating.

services.name: Anniversary bonus
services.detail: 6,000 Rapid Rewards points automatically deposited every account anniversary — double the Plus card's 3,000.

services.name: Annual flight discount
services.detail: One 15% off promo code each cardmember year, delivered within 15 days of your anniversary; excludes Basic fares.

services.name: Inflight purchase credit
services.detail: 25% back as a statement credit on Southwest inflight food, drinks and Wi-Fi purchases.

services.name: Partner perks (through 2027)
services.detail: 3-month complimentary Instacart+ trial (see the Instacart credit above), 12-month complimentary DashPass (activate by Dec 31, 2027), and 3× Rapid Rewards points on Lyft rides through Dec 2027 — ahead of Plus's 2×, behind Priority's 5×.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on international spend.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns Rapid Rewards on their spend (into your account)
Own card number, contributes to the account's Companion Pass and A-List progress
Free-bag and boarding perks come from being booked on the same reservation, not from authorized-user status

additional_cards.note: Authorized users are free on all three Southwest personal cards. The free checked bag and Group 5 boarding apply to whoever is booked on the same reservation as the cardholder (up to 8 people) — you don't need to add someone as an authorized user for them to get it, just book them on your itinerary.

timeline.text: Southwest ended free checked bags for all passengers ($35 first bag / $45 second bag on most fares) — this card's free first bag went from a redundant perk to a real one overnight.
Fee raised $99 → $149; gained the free checked bag, Preferred seat selection, Group 5 boarding, A-List tier-point earning, and partner perks (Instacart+, DashPass, Lyft points).
Legacy benefits from the old card structure — 2 EarlyBird Check-In credits and 2× points on internet/cable/phone — were discontinued.
